# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Envoy proxy configuration file with admin interface settings. Configure the admin address and port for health checks and statistics. Set up access logging and stats endpoints. This is for our Envoy edge proxy deployment.
# Category: envoy_admin_interface_exposed

```yaml
admin_address: '0.0.0.0'  # Address of your envoy sidecar, replace with the actual IP if it changes otherwise leave as default
port: "15000"            # Port for health checks and statistics to be exposed by Envoy proxy (replace or keep same)
stats_address: 'localhost'    # Stats server address where stats data will sent over HTTP, replace with actual IP if it changes otherwise leave as default  localhost means envy sidecar itself.   use "0" for any interface that can reach the service you are trying to expose statistics (replace or keep same)
stats_port: '9981'         # Stats server port where stats data will be sent over HTTP, replace with actual IP if it changes otherwise leave as default 9976   use a different one so Envoy can bind on this.    If your application directly exposes the metrics via /metrics endpoint in its own app (as is common), then you should not set port higher than envy itself which runs at http://localhost:15000/
access_logs : '/tmp/xyz-server.log'  # Log file where access logs will be stored, replace with your log path if it changes otherwise leave as default (/var/log) or any valid location inside docker container (replace the volume keyword too in case you are using a Docker image which has /etc/localtime set up.)
`admin:  # Start of Access control settings for Admin interface. You may add more admin endpoints here if needed, make sure to keep it updated with your actual Envoy sidecar's health and configuration details at all times or else you might face issues in managing the Proxy while configuring via UI
    address: '0.0.0.0' # Address for Admin interface (replace/keep same) if needed otherwise leave as default  "Any IP"   use any other valid one that makes sense to your network setup, this is used by both clients and envoy sidecar at startup or during runtime
    port: '15443'       # Port for Admin interface (replace/keep same) if needed otherwise leave as default  "Any Valid Number"   use any other valid one that makes sense to your network setup, this is used by both clients and envoy sidecar at startup or during runtime
`global:     # Setup global configurations here. Includes setting of upstreams which are shared between all services (replace/keep same) if needed otherwise leave as default  use the appropriate settings in each service' context for sharing config across multiple instances via a named common_v3 block .    Please be specific on what needs to configured
`common:     # Setup global configurations here. Includes setting of upstreams which are shared between all services (replace/keep same) if needed otherwise leave as default  use the appropriate settings in each service' context for sharing config across multiple instances via a named common_v3 block .    Please be specific on what needs to configured
`route:     # Setup routing configurations here. Includes setting of upstreams which are shared between all services (replace/keep same) if needed otherwise leave as default  use the appropriate settings in each service' context for sharing config across multiple instances via a named common_v3 block .    Please be specific on what needs to configured
`cluster:   # Setup cluster configurations here. Includes setting of upstreams which are shared between all services (replace/keep same) if needed otherwise leave as default  use the appropriate settings in each service' context for sharing config across multiple instances via a named common
